subject: Update detmVers script for snapshots
commit_message:
Update detmVers script for snapshots

The current script assumes that snapshot versions are always available.
However the Linux Foundation has now implemented a policy of deleting
snapshots on no-master branches that are older than a certain age. THis
breaks our CSITs.

This updated script copes with this by checking if a snapshot is
avaialable and, if the snapshot is not available, the script falls back
to the released version.

This script will be needed on other branches besides master as well.

CLAMP TOSCA Control Loop was not in Honolulu and the CLAMP CSITs ran
separately in the CLAMP repo. We ported the CSITs over to the
policy/docker repo in Istanbul. Therefore, we need to disable the CLAMP
CSITs in policy/docker in honolulu

Issue-ID: POLICY-4233
Change-Id: I83f0a6816942d5bfaef7c22fc69625f40ae74d23
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

@liam - 
I think, I'll need you to regenarate the certificate stores that you produced.   I saw errors in previous runs where certificate issues when going to dmaap  (the simulator).   
I tried to grab a recent set of OOM stores to get new certs, but they are not doing the trick.   
Then I noticed that in the certificates that you added for the other components:  ks.jks and policy-trustore, they are different than the OOM ones, they have extra stuff and a different root ca.
One particular difference of the ks.jks is that the SAN in this keystore is missing an entry for
DNS Name: policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org
which is present in the original keystore and it is the hostname that docker-compose gives it to the simulator which does the dmaap that was giving trouble.   
If you could copy the stores, and place them into csit/config/drools-apps/custom/ as well, hopefully we could get a little further ?

I believe the issue is with the certificate stores, the simulator's host name that does the functions of dmaap, aai, etc .. is host policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org.    It is serving a certificate for drools-pdp that does not match its hostname.   Looking at the certificate store, the DNS supported by the certificate (SAN section) are:  

DNS Name: policy 
DNS Name: drools 
DNS Name: drools.onap 
DNS Name: policy-apex-pdp 
DNS Name: policy-apex-pdp.onap 
DNS Name: policy-api 
DNS Name: policy-api.onap 
DNS Name: policy-distribution 
DNS Name: policy-distribution.onap 
DNS Name: policy-pap 
DNS Name: policy-pap.onap 
DNS Name: policy-xacml-pdp 
DNS Name: policy-xacml-pdp.onap 
DNS Name: simulator 
DNS Name: aai-sim 
DNS Name: grpc-sim 
DNS Name: sdnc-sim 
DNS Name: so-sim 
DNS Name: vfc-sim 
DNS Name: message-router 

It does *not* contain this entry:   DNS: policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org.

When I compared it with the old certificate store, it had that entry, here is the old one:

RFC 822 Name: mark.d.manager@people.osaaf.com 
DNS Name: policy 
DNS Name: policy-apex-pdp 
DNS Name: policy-apex-pdp.onap 
DNS Name: policy-api 
DNS Name: policy-api.onap 
DNS Name: policy-distribution 
DNS Name: policy-distribution.onap 
DNS Name: policy-drools-pdp 
DNS Name: policy-drools-pdp.onap 
DNS Name: policy-pap 
DNS Name: policy-pap.onap 
DNS Name: policy-xacml-pdp 
DNS Name: policy-xacml-pdp.onap 
DNS Name: policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org

Note the very last entry.
In the truststore I see that there are some new entries, probably these new ones are generated from some other root certificates different than the AAF provided ones.

There are several potential paths for solutions, in order of simplicity I think a priori.

1- regenerate the certificate stores but with the added DNS entry policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org and retry.
2- try to change the hostname of the simulator to something else other than policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org, perhaps just use "simulator" which is a valid DNS entry in the new store.
3- have the simulator use http instead of https via configuration (affects other components)>
4- have drools apps pull all configuration out in the custom directory and configure to use http.

Not sure how difficult is to regenerate the certificate stores, but if not difficult could they be regenerated containing the additional SAN entry:
DNS Name: policy.api.simpledemo.onap.org ?
